mutations.change: Keep the current state instead of following the relational next-state foreign key when state tics expire.
mutations.reason: A focused exact transition distinguishes table-authored advancement from a procedural or client-selected state.

mutations.change: Allow breadth-first sound reachability to traverse a line whose reviewed sound-block property is enabled.
mutations.reason: The blocked multihop fixture must remain unreachable even though the underlying sector graph stays connected.

mutations.change: Continue to exact intercept processing even when the source and target sector pair has its REJECT bit set.
mutations.reason: The negative-filter fixture has no earlier intercept and therefore exposes an implementation that ignores REJECT.

mutations.change: Declare an unset REJECT pair visible without testing the ordered exact blocking linedef intercepts.
mutations.reason: The visible-bit fixture contains a blocker before the target and independently requires an occluded result.

mutations.change: Reverse diagonal and axial chase preference while preserving each candidate direction and collision probe.
mutations.reason: The unblocked diagonal fixture pins the reviewed deterministic preference rather than accepting any closer move.

mutations.change: Ignore the stable collision result and advance a chasing monster through a blocking wall or solid actor.
mutations.reason: Focused blocked chase replays for every type require unchanged coordinates and stable blocker identity.

mutations.change: Apply melee damage without the relational range bound while retaining the same target and damage random read.
mutations.reason: Paired inside and outside range fixtures isolate the required exact range boundary for melee attack states.

mutations.change: Let monster hitscan damage a target behind the nearest exact blocking intercept on the attack ray.
mutations.reason: The occluded focused replay has a target but requires zero damage and no penetration beyond the blocker.

mutations.change: Spawn a missile with the player as owner instead of the attacking monster while leaving its coordinates unchanged.
mutations.reason: The expected projectile row and later collision attribution pin the stable authoritative monster owner.

mutations.change: Consume a damage random value before determining that range or exact line of sight rejects the attack.
mutations.reason: A rejected attack fixture fixes both unchanged player state and an unchanged database RNG cursor.

mutations.change: Enter the pain state after every nonlethal hit regardless of the relational pain chance and random value.
mutations.reason: The paired pain and resistance cases use exact reviewed random values on both sides of each threshold.

mutations.change: Suppress the pain transition after a qualifying nonlethal damage event while still decrementing health.
mutations.reason: Every monster type has a focused qualifying pain replay that pins state, timing, target, and event.

mutations.change: Suppress the relational ammo or weapon pickup drop for lethal damage to a configured former-human type.
mutations.reason: Focused death replays pin exact type, owner, position, and single creation for both configured droppers.

mutations.change: Create a generic pickup for monsters whose relational definition intentionally has no death drop.
mutations.reason: Focused lethal replays for spectre, imp, and demon require no drop while still producing a corpse.

mutations.change: Emit death credit and create a second corpse transition when damage is applied again to a dead monster.
mutations.reason: The repeated lethal damage fixture fixes one death event, one corpse, and no repeat kill credit.

mutations.change: Advance monsters and assign event ordinals in physical insertion order instead of stable ascending mobj identifier.
mutations.reason: Permuted input rows must yield byte-identical ordered state and event output from one prior snapshot.

mutations.change: Let later actors observe same-tic state mutations made by an earlier actor rather than the prior-tic snapshot.
mutations.reason: The coupled-target fixture requires simultaneous immutable-input advancement and stable event ordering.

mutations.change: Choose pain or direction with host randomness instead of the session database random table and cursor.
mutations.reason: Repeated full lifecycle replays for every type must have identical rows, cursors, events, and hashes.

mutations.change: Advance monster state durations according to elapsed wall time rather than exactly one logical database tic.
mutations.reason: Identical command sequences under different execution delays must retain identical state timing and hashes.

mutations.change: Select or update an actor by mobj identifier without the owning session token in the relational predicate.
mutations.reason: Two-session decoys share identifiers and require all state, target, damage, and events to remain isolated.

mutations.change: Commit monster advancement independently instead of participating in the owning deterministic tic transaction.
mutations.reason: An injected later failure must atomically roll back monster state, player damage, projectiles, drops, and events.

mutations.change: Dispatch attacks and drops with CASE branches containing the five reviewed numeric monster type identifiers.
mutations.reason: The contract requires independently authored relational definitions and rejects embedded fixture-answer dispatch.

mutations.change: Sample points along the ray instead of ordering exact rational line intercept parameters with stable ties.
mutations.reason: A translated endpoint-tie fixture preserves exact visibility but changes the result of finite point sampling.

mutations.change: Revisit already seen sectors during sound traversal so a cyclic sound graph never reaches a bounded fixed point.
mutations.reason: The cyclic graph fixture requires each sector to be visited at most once and a deterministic bounded result.
